---
jupyter: python3
format: 
  pdf:
    echo: false
    geometry:
      - top=5mm        
      - left=5mm
      - right=5mm
      - bottom=20mm
      - headheight=35mm 
      - headsep=5mm 
      - includehead=true 
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{tabularx}
          \usepackage{multirow}
          \usepackage{graphicx}
          \usepackage{xcolor}
          \usepackage{fancyhdr}
          \usepackage{lastpage}
          \usepackage{multicol}
          \usepackage{ifthen}

          \pagestyle{fancy}
          \fancyhf{} 
          \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
          \newcounter{score}

          \fancyhead[C]{
            \noindent
            \bgroup
            \def\arraystretch{1.4}
            \begin{tabularx}{\textwidth}{|p{5.5cm}|p{1.8cm}|X|p{1.2cm}|p{1.8cm}|}
            \hline
            \multirow{5}{5.5cm}{
                \vspace*{1mm}
                \includegraphics[width=4.5cm]{logo} \\
                \fontsize{6pt}{7pt}\selectfont
                \textbf{CIVIL \& ENVIRONMENTAL ENGINEERING,}\\
                SKEMPTON BUILDING, IMPERIAL COLLEGE\\
                LONDON, SOUTH KENSINGTON CAMPUS,\\
                LONDON SW7 2AZ.\\[3mm]
                \normalsize\textbf{CALCULATION SHEET}
            }
            & \textbf{Job No.} & & \textbf{Sheet} & \textbf{Ref} \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \textbf{Job Title} & Steel Design Coursework & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\thepage\ of \pageref{LastPage}} \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \multicolumn{4}{p{\dimexpr\textwidth-5.5cm-8\tabcolsep-5\arrayrulewidth\relax}|}{
                \textbf{Subject} \newline
                \vspace*{1mm}
                \centerline{Left Edge Secondary Beam Calculations}
                \vspace*{1mm}
            } \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \multirow{2}{*}{\textbf{Client}} & \textbf{Made by} \quad Pablo Peng & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\textbf{Date} \quad 29/03/2023} \\ \cline{3-5}
            & & \textbf{Checked by}  Pablo Peng \quad & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\textbf{Date}} \\ \hline
            \end{tabularx}
            \egroup
          }
---
\Large
\linespread{1.4}
\selectfont 

```{python}
import math

# Define beam type and section
beam = 'Secondary' 
Type = 'Edge' # 'Internal' or 'Edge' or 'Slanted'
section = '457 x 191x 161'

%precision 2
# Floor area and height
lx = 1500
ly = 13500
ls = math.sqrt(lx**2 + ly**2)
H = 6000

# floor loads
gt_f = 0.7
gs_f = 2.63
gc_f = 0.5
gste_f = 0.3
gser_f = 1.4

qpar_f = 1.0
qimp_f = 4.0
if Type == 'Edge':
  qcl = 1.2
elif Type == 'Slanted':
  qcl = 1.2
else:
  qcl = 0.0

# ULS floor load
Gtyp_f = gt_f + gs_f + gc_f + gste_f + gser_f
Qtyp_f = qpar_f + qimp_f
wtyp_f = 1.35*Gtyp_f + 1.5*Qtyp_f
qtyp_f = wtyp_f*lx*1e-3
qclad = 1.5*qcl*H*1e-3
if Type == 'Edge':
  qtotal = qclad + qtyp_f
  beamscore = 0
elif Type == 'Slanted':
  qtotal = 0.5*wtyp_f*lx*ly/ls*1e-3 + qclad
  beamscore = 1
else:
  qtotal = qtyp_f
  beamscore = 0

# Moment and shear
if Type == 'Slanted':
  lb = ls
else:
  lb = ly
Med = qtotal*(lb*1e-3)**2/8
Ved = qtotal*(lb*1e-3)/2
```

# 
#
#
#
#
#

# Floor Loads
\begin{multicols}{2}
\begin{description}
    \item[]$G_{tiles}$ = `{python} gt_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{steel}$ = `{python} gste_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{ceiling}$ = `{python} gc_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{slab}$ = `{python} gs_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$G_{services}$ = `{python} gser_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$Q_{partitions}$ = `{python} qpar_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$Q_{imposed}$ = `{python} qimp_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]$Q_{cladding}$ = `{python} qcl` $kN/m^2$
\end{description}
\end{multicols}

# ULS floor uniform distributed load
\begin{description}
    \item[]Typical dead load, $G_{typ}$ = $G_{tiles} + G_{steel} + G_{ceiling} + G_{slab} + G_{services}$ = `{python} Gtyp_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]Typical imposed load, $Q_{typ}$ = $Q_{partitions} + Q_{imposed} $ = `{python} Qtyp_f` $kN/m^2$
    \item[]Typical area load, $W_{typ}$ = $1.35\cdot G_{typ} + 1.5 \cdot Q_{typ}$ = `{python} wtyp_f` $kN/m^2$
\end{description}

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
# Axial and bending loads on the beam
\begin{description}
    \item[]$L_{x}$ = `{python} lx` $mm$
    \item[]$L_{y}$ = `{python} ly` $mm$
    \item[]Height of between floor, $H$ = `{python} H` $mm$
\end{description}

\setcounter{score}{`{python} int(beamscore)`}
\ifthenelse{\value{score} > 0}{
\begin{description}
  \item[]Length of the slanted beam, $L_{s}$ = $\sqrt{L_{x}^{2} + L_{y}^{2}}$ = `{python} ls` $mm$
  \item[]Length of the beam, $L_{b}$ = $L_{s}$ = `{python} lb` $mm$
  \item[]UDL, $q_{Total}$ = $\frac{0.5\cdot W_{typ}\cdot L_{x}\cdot L_{y}}{L_{s}}$ + $1.5\cdot Q_{cladding}\cdot H$ = `{python} qtotal` $kN/m$
\end{description}}{
\begin{description}
    \item[]Length of the beam, $L_{b}$ = $L_{y}$ = `{python} lb` $mm$
    \item[]UDL, $q_{Total}$ = $W_{typ}\cdot L_{x}$ + $1.5\cdot Q_{cladding}\cdot H$ = `{python} qtotal` $kN/m$
\end{description}}
\begin{description}
    \item[]Moment, $M_{ed}$ = $\frac{q_{Total}\cdot L_{b}^{2}}{8}$ = `{python} Med` $kNm$
    \item[]Shear, $V_{ed}$ = $\frac{q_{Total}\cdot L_{b}}{2}$ = `{python} Ved` $kN$
\end{description}

```{python}
import pandas as pd

df_all = pd.read_csv('section_mm.csv')
if df_all.columns[0] == 'Unnamed: 0':
  df_all = df_all.rename(columns = {'Unnamed: 0' : 'Section Profile'})

# Scientific notation formatter
def sci(value):
    exp_num = math.floor(math.log10(abs(value)))
    base = value / (10 ** exp_num)
    return value, base, exp_num

#material
fu= 500.0
fy = 355.0
E = 210000
G = 81000
fck = 30.0
Ecm = 33000

#section properties
df = df_all[df_all['Section Profile'] == section]
h = float(df['h (mm)'].iloc[0])
b = float(df['b (mm)'].iloc[0])
tw = float(df['tw (mm)'].iloc[0])
tf = float(df['tf (mm)'].iloc[0])
d = float(df['d (mm)'].iloc[0])
r= float(df['r(mm)'].iloc[0])
A, A1, A2 = sci(float(df['A (mm^2)'].iloc[0]))
Iy, Iy1, Iy2 = sci(float(df['Iy (mm^4)'].iloc[0]))
Iz, Iz1, Iz2 = sci(float(df['Iz (mm^4)'].iloc[0]))
Ry = float(df['Ry (mm)'].iloc[0])
Rz = float(df['Rz (mm)'].iloc[0])
Wey, Wey1, Wey2 = sci(float(df['W_el,y (mm^3)'].iloc[0]))
Wez, Wez1, Wez2 = sci(float(df['W_el,z (mm^3)'].iloc[0]))
Wpy, Wpy1, Wpy2 = sci(float(df['W_pl,y (mm3)'].iloc[0]))
Wpz, Wpz1, Wpz2 = sci(float(df['W_pl,z (mm^3)'].iloc[0]))
Iw, Iw1, Iw2 = sci(float(df['I_w (mm^6)'].iloc[0]))
It, It1, It2 = sci(float(df['I_T (mm^4)'].iloc[0]))

ratio = h/b
if ratio > 1.2:
  if tf <= 40:
    ay = 0.21
    az = 0.34
  else:
    ay = 0.34
    az = 0.49
else:
  if tf <= 100:
    ay = 0.34
    az = 0.49
  else:
    ay = 0.70
    az = 0.70
```

# Material Properties
\begin{description}
  \item[]Ultimate strength: $f_{u}$ = `{python} fu` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$  
  \item[]Yield strength: $f_{y}$ = `{python} fy` $N \cdot mm^{-2}$  
  \item[]Young modulus: E = `{python} E` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$  
  \item[]Shear modulus: G = `{python} G` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$
  \item[]Concrete strength: $f_{ck}$ = `{python} fck` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$
  \item[]Concrete Young modulus: $E_{cm}$ = `{python} Ecm` $N\cdot mm^{-2}$
\end{description}

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
# Section Properties of UKB `{python} section`
\begin{multicols}{2}
\begin{description}
  \item[] Overall depth, $h$ = `{python} h` mm  
  \item[] Width of section, $b$ = `{python} b` mm  
  \item[] Web thickness, $t_{w}$ = `{python} tw` mm  
  \item[] Flange thickness, $t_{f}$ = `{python} tf` mm  
  \item[] Depth between fillet, $h_{w}$ = `{python} d` mm  
  \item[] Root radius, $r$ = `{python} r` mm  
  \item[] Area of section, $A$ = `{python} A1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} A2`}$ $mm^{2}$ 
  \item[] 2nd moment Y-Y, $I_{y}$ = `{python} Iy1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Iy2`}$ $mm^{4}$  
  \item[] 2nd moment Z-Z, $I_{z}$ = `{python} Iz1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Iz2`}$ $mm^{4}$  
  \item[] Radius of gyration Y-Y, $i_{y}$ = `{python} Ry` mm  
  \item[] Radius of gyration Z-Z, $i_{z}$ = `{python} Rz` mm  
  \item[] Elastic modulus Y-Y, $W_{el,y}$ = `{python} Wey1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Wey2`}$ $mm^{3}$  
  \item[] Elastic modulus Z-Z, $W_{el,z}$ = `{python} Wez1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Wez2`}$ $mm^{3}$  
  \item[] Plastic modulus Y-Y, $W_{pl,y}$ = `{python} Wpy1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Wpy2`}$ $mm^{3}$  
  \item[] Plastic modulus Z-Z, $W_{pl,z}$ = `{python} Wpz1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Wpz2`}$ $mm^{3}$  
  \item[] Warping constant, $I_{w}$ = `{python} Iw1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} Iw2`}$ $mm^{6}$  
  \item[] Torsional constant, $I_{t}$ = `{python} It1` $\cdot 10^{`{python} It2`}$   $mm^{4}$  
\end{description}
\end{multicols}

```{python}
ep = math.sqrt(235/fy)
crf = 9*ep
crw = 72*ep
cw = d
c1 = cw/tw
cf = (b-tw-2*r)/2
c2 = cf/tf
```

# Section Classification

According to EN 1993-1-1:2022 Eq 5.1, $\epsilon$ = $\sqrt{\frac{235}{f_{y}}}$ = `{python} ep` 

\begin{multicols}{2}
\begin{description}
  \item[]$c_{w}$ = $\frac{(b-t_{w}-2\cdot r)}{2}$ = `{python} cw`  
  \item[] $\frac{c_{w}}{t_{w}}$ = `{python} c1`
  \item[]Class 1 limit for web, 72 $\epsilon $ = `{python} crw`
  \item[]$c_{f}$ = $h_{w}$ = `{python} cf` 
  \item[]$\frac{c_{f}}{t_{f}}$ = `{python} c2`
  \item[]Class 1 limit for flange, 9 $\epsilon$ = `{python} crf`
\end{description}  
\end{multicols}

Since `{python} c1` < `{python} crw` and `{python} c2` < `{python} crf`, the section belongs to class 1 

```{python}
# Bending Resistance of the fully connected composite beam
gammac = 1.5
a_c = 0.85
Le = ly
beff = Le/4
ht = 180
SD = ly/(h+ht)
hp = 80 + 15
if beam == 'Primary':
  hc = ht -hp/2
else:
  hc = ht - hp
fcd = a_c*fck/gammac
Ncf = fcd*beff*hc*1e-3
Napl = fy*A*1e-3
Nac = Napl - Ncf
if Nac > 0:
  x = ht + Nac*1e3/(2*fy*b)
  Mplrd = (Napl*1e3*(h/2 + ht - hc/2) - Nac*1e3*((x-ht)/2 + ht - hc/2))*1e-6
  nac = 1
else:
  x = Napl*1e3/(fcd*beff)
  Mplrd = (Napl*1e3*(h/2 + ht - x/2))*1e-6
  nac = 0
if Mplrd > Med:
  output_moment = 'the fully connected composite beam is safe in bending'
else:
  output_moment = 'the fully connected composite beam is not safe in bending'
```

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
# Bending Resistance of the Composite Beam (Full connection)
\begin{description}
  \item[]Effective width, $b_{eff}$ = $\frac{L_{y}}{4}$ = `{python} beff` mm
  \item[]Factored strength of concrete, $f_{cd}$ = `{python} fcd` $N/mm²$
  \item[]Depth of steel, $h$ = `{python} h` mm
  \item[]Depth of concrete and comflor slab, $h_{t}$ = `{python} ht` mm
  \item[]Span to depth ratio, $\frac{S}{D}$ = $\frac{L_{y}}{h + h_{t}}$ = `{python} SD`
  \item[]Height of the comflor slab, $h_{p}$ = `{python} hp` mm
  \item[]Height of the concrete in compression, $h_{c}$ = $h_{t} - h_{comflor}$ = `{python} hc` mm
  \item[]Total strength of concrete, $N_{cf}$ = $f_{cd}\cdot b_{eff} \cdot h_{c}$ = `{python} Ncf` kN
  \item[]Plastic strength of the steel, $N_{apl}$ = $f_{y}\cdot A$ = `{python} Napl` kN
\end{description}

\setcounter{score}{`{python} int(nac)`}
\ifthenelse{\value{score}>0}{
\begin{description}
  \item[]$N_{cf}$ is less than the $N_{apl}$, the neutral axis is within the steel section, 
  \item[]$N_{ac}$ = $N_{apl} - N_{cf}$ = `{python} Nac` kN
  \item[]Neutral axis depth, $x$ = $h_t + \frac{N_{ac}}{2\cdot f_{y}\cdot b}$ = `{python} x` mm
  \item[]Design moment capacity, $M_{rd}$ = $N_{apl}\cdot(\frac{h}{2} + h_{t} - \frac{h_{c}}{2}) - N_{ac} \cdot (\frac{x-h_t}{2} + h_{t} - \frac{h_{c}}{2})$ = `{python} Mplrd` kN·m
\end {description}}{
\begin{description}
  \item[]$N_{cf}$ is greater than the $N_{apl}$, the neutral axis is within the concrete section, 
  \item[]Neutral axis depth, $x$ = $\frac{N_{apl}}{f_{cd}\cdot b_{eff}}$ = `{python} x` mm
  \item[]Design moment capacity, $M_{rd}$ = $N_{apl}\cdot(\frac{h}{2} + h_{t} - \frac{x}{2})$ = `{python} Mplrd` kN·m
\end{description}}

 Therefore `{python} output_moment`
```{python}
# Shear Resistance of the stud shear connectors
gammav = 1.25
a = 1
dstud = 19.0
b0 = 145
hp = 60
hsc = 95
nr =2
spacing = 300
pstud = (0.29*a*dstud**2*(fck*Ecm)**0.5)*1e-3/gammav
pcon = (0.8*math.pi*dstud**2*fu)*1e-3/(4*gammav)
prdi = 0.7*min(pstud, pcon)
kt = min((0.7*b0)/(nr**0.5*hp)*((hsc/hp)-1), 0.7)
prd = prdi*kt
lcrs = ly/2
nstud = 2*lcrs/spacing
vstud = prd*nstud
eta_s = vstud/Ncf
etas_slip = 1-(355/fy)*(1.433 - 0.054*lx*1e-3)
etas_moment = 0.4*(Med/Mplrd)
eta_final = max(eta_s, etas_slip, etas_moment, 0.28)

# Partial Connection moment(simplified method)
Mplard = Wpy*fy*1e-6
Mrd = Mplard + eta_final*(Mplrd - Mplard)
if Mrd > Med:
  output_partial = 'The partial connected composite beam is safe in bending'
else:
  output_partial = 'The partial connected composite beam is not safe in bending'
```

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
# Shear Resistance of the Stud Shear Connectors
\begin{description}
  \item[]$\alpha$ = `{python} a`, $\gamma_{v}$ = `{python} gammav`, $d_{stud}$ = `{python} dstud` mm
  \item[]Stud Shear Resistance $P_{stud}$ = $\frac{0.29\cdot \alpha \cdot d_{stud}^{2} \cdot (f_{ck}\cdot E_{cm})^{0.5}}{\gamma_{v}}$ = `{python} pstud` kN
  \item[]Concrete Shear Resistance $P_{concrete}$ = $\frac{0.8\cdot \pi \cdot d_{stud}^{2} \cdot f_{u}}{4\cdot \gamma_{v}}$ = `{python} pcon` kN --- (EC4 Clause 6.6.4.2)
  \item[]Unfactored Stud Shear Resistance $P_{rd,unfactored}$ = $0.7\cdot min(P_{stud}, P_{concrete})$ = `{python} prdi` kN
  \item[]$b_{0}$ = `{python} b0` mm, $h_{sc}$ = `{python} hsc` mm
  \item[]Shear Connector Factor $k_{t}$ = $min(\frac{0.70\cdot b_{0}}{n_{r}^{0.5}\cdot h_{p}}\cdot(\frac{h_{sc}}{h_{p}}-1), 0.7)$ = `{python} kt` --- (EC4 Clause 6.6.1.2)
  \item[]Factored Stud Shear resistance,$P_{rd}$ = $P_{rd,unfactored}\cdot k_{t}$ = `{python} prd` kN
  \item[]Critical length of the beam, $l_{crs}$ = $\frac{l_{y}}{2}$ = `{python} lcrs` mm
  \item[]Number of stud shear connectors, $n_{stud}$ = $\frac{2\cdot l_{crs}}{spacing}$ = `{python} nstud`
  \item[]Total Shear resistance of shear connectors, $V_{stud}$ = $p_{rd}\cdot n_{stud}$ = `{python} vstud` kN
  \item[]Shear connection efficiency, $\eta_{s}$ = $\frac{V_{stud}}{N_{cf}}$ = `{python} eta_s`
  \item[]Shear connection efficiency limit based on slip, $\eta_{s1}$ = $1-(\frac{355}{f_{y}})\cdot(1.433 - 0.054\cdot l_{x})$ = `{python} etas_slip`
  \item[]Shear connection efficiency limit based on moment, $\eta_{s2}$ = $0.4\cdot\frac{M_{ed}}{M_{plrd}}$ = `{python} etas_moment`
  \item[]Final shear connection efficiency, $\eta_{final}$ = $max(\eta_{s}, \eta_{s1}, \eta_{s2}, 0.28)$ = `{python} eta_final`
\end{description}

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
# Bending Resistance of the Partially Connected Composite Beam (Simplified Method)
\begin{description}
  \item[]Assuming the neutral axis depth is at the steel section (mostly true)
  \item[]Plastic moment capacity of the steel section, $M_{plard}$ = $W_{pl,y}\cdot f_{y}$ = `{python} Mplard` N·mm
  \item[]$M_{rd}$ = $M_{plard} + \eta_{final}\cdot(M_{plrd} - M_{plard})$ = `{python} Mrd` N·mm --- (EC4 Clause 6.2.1.3)
  \item[]Design moment, $M_{ed}$ = `{python} Med` kN·m
  \item[]Therefore, `{python} output_partial`
\end{description}

```{python}
# Shear Resistance of steel section
gamm0 = 1.0
eta = 1.0
Ashear = min(A - (2*b*tf) + (tw+2*r), eta*d*tw)
Vplrd = (Ashear*fy)*1e-3/(gamm0*math.sqrt(3))
if Vplrd > Ved:
  outputv = 'The composite beam is safe in shear'
else:
  outputv = 'The composite beam is not safe in shear'

# Shear resistance of the concrete 
vced = vstud*1e3/(2*hc*lcrs)
vfcd = 0.6*(1-(fck/250))*fcd
theta = 26.5
radiantstheta = math.radians(theta)
vcrd = vfcd*(math.sin(radiantstheta))*math.cos(radiantstheta)
if vcrd > vced:
  outputvcon = 'The concrete is safe in shear'
else:
  outputvcon = 'The concrete is not safe in shear'

# Longitudinal shear resistance of composite beam
fyd = fu/1.15
mesh_ed = 1e3*vced*hc*math.tan(radiantstheta)/fyd
mesh_rd = 252
if mesh_rd > mesh_ed:
  outputmesh = 'The shear mesh is sufficient'
else:
  outputmesh = 'The shear mesh is not sufficient'
```

# Shear Resistance of the steel section
\begin{description}
  \item[]According to EN 1993-1-1:2022 Clause 8.23
  \item[]Shear Area $A_{shear}$ = $min(A - (2\cdot b \cdot t_{f}) + (t_{w}+2\cdot r), \eta \cdot h_{w} \cdot t_{w})$ = `{python} Ashear` $mm^{2}$ 
  \item[]Partial Safety Factor for shear, $\gamma_{v}$ = 1.25 
  \item[]Shear resistance of the beam, $V_{pl,rd}$= $\frac{A_{shear}\cdot f_{y}}{\gamma_{0}\cdot\sqrt{3}}$ = `{python} Vplrd` kN
\end{description}

# Crushing resistance of the concrete
\begin{description}
  \item[]Shear stress in the composite structure, $v_{ed}$ = $\frac{V_{stud}\cdot 10^{3}}{2\cdot h_{c} \cdot l_{crs}}$ = `{python} vced` N/mm²
  \item[]Design shear strength of the concrete, $vf_{cd}$ = $0.6(1-\frac{f_{ck}}{250})\cdot f_{cd}$ = `{python} vfcd` N/mm²
  \item[]Angle of inclination of the concrete struts, $\theta$ = `{python} theta` °
\end{description}

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
\begin{description}
  \item[]Shear resistance of the concrete, $v_{crd}$ = $vf_{cd}\cdot\sin(\theta)\cdot\cos(\theta)$ = `{python} vcrd` N/mm²
  \item[]Therefore, `{python} outputvcon`
\end{description}

# Longitudinal shear resistance of the composite beam
\begin{description}
  \item[]Required mesh = $\frac{v_{ed}\cdot h_{c}}{f_{yd}\cdot \cot(\theta)}$ = `{python} mesh_ed` mm²/m
  \item[]Provided mesh = `{python} mesh_rd` mm²/m
  \item[]Therefore, `{python} outputmesh`
\end{description}

```{python}
n0 = E/Ecm
n = 2*n0
zg = h/2 + ht
Adefls = A*(zg - hc)
Adeflc = beff*hc**2/(2*n) 
axis = (n * A * zg + 0.5*beff*hc**2)/(n * A + beff*hc)
Inertia = Iy + A*(zg-axis)**2 + (beff*hc/n)*((hc**2/12)+(axis-hc/2)**2)
qsls = Qtyp_f*lx*1e-3
deflec_comp = 5*qsls*ly**4/(384*E*Inertia)
deflec_steel = 5*qsls*ly**4/(384*E*Iy)
deflec_partial = deflec_comp + 0.3*(1-eta_final)*(deflec_steel - deflec_comp)
deflec_lim = ly/360
if deflec_partial > deflec_lim:
  outputdeflec = 'The partially connected composite beam does not satisfy the deflection limit'
else:
  outputdeflec = 'The partially connected composite beam satisfies the deflection limit'
```

# Deflection check of composite beam
\begin{description}
  \item[]Modular ratio, $n_{0}$ = $\frac{E_{steel}}{E_{concrete}}$ = `{python} n0`
  \item[]Lever arm the steel section, $z_{g}$ = $\frac{h}{2} + h_{t}$ = `{python} zg` mm
  \item[] $A\cdot(z_{g} - h_{c})$ = `{python} Adefls` mm³
  \item[]n = $2\cdot n_{0}$ = `{python} n`
  \item[]$\frac{b_{eff}\cdot h_{c}^{2}}{2\cdot n}$ = `{python} Adeflc` mm³
  \item[]Neutral axis, $x$ = $\frac{n\cdot A\cdot z_{g} + 0.5\cdot b_{eff}\cdot h_{c}^{2}}{n\cdot A + b_{eff}\cdot h_{c}}$ = `{python} axis` mm
  \item[]$I$ = $I_{y} + A\cdot (z_{g}-x)^{2} + \frac{b_{eff}\cdot h_{c}}{n}\cdot(\frac{h_{c}^{2}}{12}+(x-\frac{h_{c}}{2})^{2})$ = `{python} Inertia` mm⁴
  \item[]$q_{sls}$ = `{python} qsls` kN/m
  \item[]Deflection of the composite beam, $\delta_{comp}$ = $\frac{5\cdot q\cdot L^{4}}{384\cdot E \cdot I}$ = `{python} deflec_comp` mm
\end{description}

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
\begin{description}
  \item[]Deflection of the steel beam, $\delta_{steel}$ = $\frac{5\cdot q\cdot L^{4}}{384\cdot E \cdot I_y}$ = `{python} deflec_steel` mm
  \item[]Deflection of composite beam, $\delta_{partial}$ = $\delta_{comp} + 0.3\cdot(1-\eta_{final})\cdot(\delta_{steel} - \delta_{comp})$ = `{python} deflec_partial` mm
  \item[]Deflection limit, $\delta_{lim}$ = $\frac{L}{360}$ = `{python} deflec_lim` mm
  \item[]`{python} outputdeflec`
\end{description}
